title: Profile
description: This schema defines the Profile part of a Service Description. See more about Profile in SDCM v.3.0.0

provider.description: The organization that offers the use of capabilities by means of the service.

consumer.description: An organizational entity that uses the service.

function.description: A type of activity describing the functionality of the service.

name.description: A name given to the function in the context of the service description.

real-world-effect.description: An ultimate purpose associated with the interaction with the service. It may be the response to a request for information or the change in the state of some entities shared between the participants in the interaction.

security-mechanism.description: A process (or a device incorporating such a process) that is used by or within a service to prevent unauthorized or accidental access, change, destruction, or loss of data.

name.description: The name of the security mechanism.

description.description: A description of the security mechanism.

category.description: The type of security mechanism that the service employs.

protocol.description: A formal document prescribing rules for secure coordination of interacting components.

access-restrictions.description: Access Restriction of the security mechanism.

policy.description: A statement that defines constraints on the behavior of a managed resource, a user or an organization.

source.description: A document from which the policy is derived or cited.

quality-of-service.description: A set of parameters that specify and measure the value of the provided service.

name.description: The name of the quality of service parameter.

value.description: The value or range of values that the quality of service parameter is expected to meet or possess.

description.description: A description of the quality of service parameter.

calculation-method.description: A description of how the quality of service parameter values are measured or calculated.

unit-of-measure.description: The unit of measure in which the quality of service parameter values are expressed.

geographical-extent.description: A specified geographic area to which the service applies.

id.description: A code or a URI that references a label in a specified scheme.

name.description: A name given to the geographical extent.

description.description: A description of the geographical extent.

source.description: A document from which the geographical extent is derived or cited.

geometry.description: A set of points in coordinate reference system used to model the geographical extent or shape.

environmental-constraint.description: A characteristic of the environment or larger system within which the service operates; examples include: capacity of existing enterprise network, firewalls, physical computing resources, etc.

name.description: A name given to the environmental constraint in the context of the service description.

description.description: A description of the environmental constraint.

service-validation.description: Validation method used to assess a service.

description.description: The description of the service validation.

status.description: The lifecycle status of a service in accordance with relevant governance policies.
